text: Being here, I am struck by the vastness and apparent quiet of the first space. The beanbags and stage suggest it is a place for gathering, yet now it is empty, filled only with the hum of possibility. It feels contemplative, almost meditative. Turning to the next scene, the colorful, stimulating wall of the R+D exhibition awakens my curiosity. I feel energized and thoughtful, drawn to the idea of innovation and human connection presented. These environments pull me in different directions: the first invites inward reflection, the second outward engagement. I feel honored to witness the intersection of solitude and creativity.
